rationale.R1.1: Positioning against prior work centrally requires framing the thesis, gap, and contribution relative to existing research lines.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation entails checking whether the claimed relationship among prior-work categories and the paper's contribution is logically coherent.
rationale.R1.4: Assessing novelty versus nearest prior work is load-bearing because the request is specifically to position the artifact against prior work.
rationale.R1.7: The task depends on adequate coverage, attribution, and characterization of relevant scholarship, though not necessarily a full bibliography audit.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty must be disclosed because claims such as firstness and nearest-prior-work distance may depend on incomplete literature knowledge.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is critical given the artifact's strong novelty language such as first, no prior work, and genuinely new.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must stay within the requested prior-work positioning task and not execute edits or broader review.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream harm avoidance applies because overstated novelty or mischaracterized prior work can mislead readers and reviewers.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability are required so any positioning responsibility remains anchored to the artifact and identifiable prior-work claims.
